diagrams.title: Initial Partition in Minimization
diagrams.description: Shows the first step of partition refinement - dividing states into accepting (F) and non-accepting (Q-F) groups
elements: Arrow showing states from Q moved to appropriate circle
diagrams.purpose: Understanding where minimization starts - can't combine accepting with non-accepting states

diagrams.title: State Distinguishability Check
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how to check if two states in same group are distinguishable by examining transitions on each input symbol
elements: Transitions on 'a' from each state to other groups
Decision tree showing split when transitions differ
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the refinement step - states going to different groups must be split

diagrams.title: Table-Filling Method
diagrams.description: Grid representation showing marking of distinguishable pairs in a triangular table
diagrams.purpose: Alternative visualization of minimization - helps understand which pairs are distinguishable

diagrams.title: Minimal DFA Construction
diagrams.description: Shows how equivalent state groups from final partition become single states in minimal DFA
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how to construct the actual minimal DFA from equivalence classes

diagrams.title: Myhill-Nerode Equivalence Classes
diagrams.description: Visual representation of equivalence classes as distinct behavioral patterns needed to recognize a language
elements: Multiple circles representing different equivalence classes
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the theoretical foundation - each class needs its own state in minimal DFA

visuals.title: Equivalence And Minimization Of Automata
visuals.description: Visual diagram for equivalence and minimization of automata
